name: Taw'ama bint Ali
title: Sister of Hussain
shortDescription: One of the daughters of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and sisters of Imam Hussain (AS) who was present at Karbala and endured captivity.
fullStory:
Taw'ama (or Jumana) bint Ali (AS) is another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS) whose presence in the Karbala caravan is mentioned in some historical and genealogical sources. She was Imam Hussain's (AS) half-sister.

When Imam Hussain (AS) began his uprising, many women and daughters of Abu Talib's family, including Taw'ama, accompanied him with complete insight. They knew this journey was full of trials and tribulations.

On Ashura, Taw'ama witnessed the martyrdom of her brothers, nephews, and the Imam's loyal companions. Alongside her noble sister Lady Zaynab (SA), she comforted the children and looked after the tents.

After the battle ended, she too was taken captive by Umar Sa'd's cruel army along with the other women of the Ahl al-Bayt, enduring the hardships of the journey to Kufa and Sham with the patience and resilience she inherited from her father Ali (AS).
quoteArabic: وَخَرَجَ الحُسَيْنُ (ع) بِأَخَواتِهِ: زَيْنَبَ، وَأُمِّ كُلْثُومٍ، وَتَوْأَمَةَ...
quote: (In historical sources:) And Hussain (AS) departed with his sisters: Zaynab, Umm Kulthum, and Taw'ama...
